ItacPersonItem.Notes: The pupil of Justus Lipsius at Louvain, Segeth went to Padua in 1597, where he was the guest of Giovan Vincenzo Pinelli. In Pinelli`s house, he befriended Galileo, who gave him a copy of his `Sidereus nuncius` to be delivered to Kepler. Segeth joined the Ricovrati in 1600. In the Academy, he delivered a speech in Latin in front of all academicians, on the subject of man`s aim in life. Apparently, his pronunciation was not so clear, however everybody praised his rhetorical skills.
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
